    92→- `∀ (V : Type*) (G : SimpleGraph V), (f : ℕ∞) ≤ G.chromaticNumber →` — every graph with chromatic number ≥ f.
    93→- `∃ (v : V) (p : G.Walk v v), p.IsCycle ∧ Odd p.length` — contains an odd cycle (via `Walk.IsCycle`).

file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/640.lean` — Erdős Problem 640

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 640`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/640`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
- [x] Includes a bibliography entry for `[Er97d]` (Erdős 1997).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos640 ... end Erdos640`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos640`).
- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

N/A — no custom `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_640` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) describing the problem.
- [x] Docstring references the source paper: `[Er97d, p.84]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks a yes/no question: "For every k ≥ 3, **does there exist** f(k) such that ...?" This is **Case 1** (yes/no question).

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`.
- [x] RHS (`P`) formalizes the question as originally asked, so `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no."
- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ k`, `∃ f`, `∀ V G`, `∃ v p`) appear **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research open` — a valid category.
- [x] Theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body (consistent with `research open`).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS 5]` attribute (combinatorics).
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single entry — trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` / `sorry` multi-line form (`:= by\n  sorry`), which is acceptable.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (no definitions present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

N/A — no variants present. Single-part problem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation increments throughout (continuation lines use 4-space base + 2-space nesting).
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `≤`, `↔`, `∧`, `ℕ`, `ℕ∞`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (longest line ~82 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass — not verified in this review.

## Mathematical Fidelity Notes

The formalization captures the problem faithfully:

- `∀ k : ℕ, 3 ≤ k → ∃ f : ℕ` — for every k ≥ 3, there exists f(k).
- `∀ (V : Type*) (G : SimpleGraph V), (f : ℕ∞) ≤ G.chromaticNumber →` — every graph with chromatic number ≥ f.
- `∃ (v : V) (p : G.Walk v v), p.IsCycle ∧ Odd p.length` — contains an odd cycle (via `Walk.IsCycle`).
- `(k : ℕ∞) ≤ (G.induce {w : V | w ∈ p.support}).chromaticNumber` — the induced subgraph on the cycle's vertices has chromatic number ≥ k.

The coercions `(f : ℕ∞)` and `(k : ℕ∞)` correctly lift natural numbers to `ℕ∞` for comparison with `chromaticNumber`, which returns in `ℕ∞`.

## Verdict

**PASS** — all applicable checklist items are satisfied. Build verification is pending.

content.text: The review verdict is **PASS**. No changes needed.
